data.body:
[G4 child-consistency — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S1 6d56: Faithful, lock-free snapshot materialization core)]

Ran the 7 cross-child interaction modes for this materialize-core child against the WHOLE eight-child roster (S1 6d56 THIS, S2 70c7 cache read/populate, S2b b73c janitor, S3 fa49 gate threading, S4 30b3 attested signing, S4b e308 claim-gate ref-coherence/ADR 0002, S5 b7fa CLI/MCP surfaces, S6 1ba5 docs). All 7 modes are CLEAN under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. EMPTY G4 findings list for this pairing.

Mode-by-mode:

1. Implicit shared state — CLEAN. This child PRODUCES the foundational shared-state contract that the rest of the snapshot stack consumes, with each producer→consumer relationship mutually named:
   - The atomic dir-rename POPULATION primitive (build into `<root>/tmp/<uuid>/`, fsync, rename into the cache path, fsync parent) is produced here and CONSUMED by S2 (70c7 "populated via S1's atomic dir-rename"; Scope "Out: materialization itself (S1)"). No double-implementation.
   - The `tmp/` residue + startup-cleanup contract is consistent across the seam: THIS child's AC3 ("a crashed materialization leaves only a tmp/ dir, cleaned on startup") names the residue; the cleanup is owned by the janitor child S2b (b73c AC3 startup sweep "rmtree tmp/* trash/*"). Producer-of-residue (S1) → sweeper (S2b) agree on the tmp/ location.
   - The flock + atomic-mkdir-fallback PORTABILITY seam is established here; S2's cross-process `flock(LOCK_EX)` on `locks/<sha>` and S2b's exclusive `flock` on `gc/lock` RIDE that seam without re-owning it.
   - The resolved immutable SHA produced by this child's resolve step is consumed downstream by S3 (re-roots the gate to the snapshot) / S4 (signs `verified-at-sha:<sha>`) / S5 (surfaces) / S6 (documents). Consistent producer→consumer chain; no value produced-without-consumer or consumed-without-producer.

2. Conflicting assumptions — CLEAN (benign-reading). This child's signature is `materialize(ref, *, source_mode) -> handle`, and its descriptive-error bullet says "attested mode fails closed, local mode still runs" — i.e. materialize() is source_mode-aware. S3 (fa49) frames local as "read the server's in-place checkout directly (no materialization, no cache)." These reconcile under the benign reading: materialize() accepts source_mode and short-circuits to the in-place read root for local (this child owns the seam definition; S3 consumes it and never builds a snapshot in local mode). The content-addressed "immutable SHA = perfect cache key (no staleness)" assumption (S2) and the reproducibility-over-identical-content assumption (S4) both rest on this child's resolve-ref→immutable-SHA + faithful (read-tree+checkout-index, export-ignore-preserving, no `git archive`) materialization — mutually consistent. No high-confidence contradiction.

3. Dependency gap — CLEAN at this child. Every upstream this child needs is internal (git fetch/resolve, tempfile, fcntl) — no unowned upstream. Every downstream hand-off this child ORIGINATES is claimed by a named sibling: the atomic-rename population → S2; the resolved-SHA snapshot dir → S3 (re-rooting) and S4 (signing); the descriptive credential MESSAGE detail → S6 (this child's bullet "see S6"; S6 AC2 claims it); the REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR/EFS-NFS flock caveat docs → S6 (AC5). The standing S3↔S4/S4b dependency gap (routing the pinned-SHA snapshot handle into plan_review/attest.py's dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/freshness re-check) is DOWNSTREAM of this child's resolved-SHA output and lives in the S3/S4/S4b seam — it does not originate at this materialize-core child, which simply produces the SHA + snapshot dir those consumers must thread. Not a gap at this pairing.

4. Scope overlap — CLEAN. This child OWNS materialization, faithful tree extraction, atomic population, the portability seam, fetch-coalescing, and descriptive fetch/auth errors. Both downstream cache children scope it out bilaterally: S2 (70c7) "Out: materialization itself (S1)"; S2b (b73c) "Out: ... materialization (S1)". This child reciprocally scopes out the cache/janitor/gate/signing/surfaces ("Out: the cache/janitor (S2), gate wiring (S3), signing (S4), surfaces (S5)"). The "coalesced git fetch" (this child) and S2's "single-flight per SHA" (coalescing materialization) are DISTINCT layers. No double-implementation.

5. Ordering violation — CLEAN. This child carries `blocks 70c7` (S2) and `blocks fa49` (S3) — i.e. S1 → {S2, S3}, coherent with the producer→consumer flow (the materialize core precedes the cache that populates from it and the gates that read it). The edges point the benign way; no contradiction. Parent's final AC only requires all-closed + green.

6. Consumer impact — CLEAN. This child's contracts (faithful byte-match committed tree incl. export-ignore; LFS-pointer detection; defined submodule handling; atomic all-or-nothing population; no-worktree; no index.lock/config.lock contention between different-SHA materializations; descriptive credential error + attested-fails-closed) are consumed without redefinition: S2 builds delete-on-last-close reads atop the atomic-rename entry; S3's gate re-rooting + ENOENT-tolerance (its AC6) ride the all-or-nothing guarantee; S4 signs over the resolved SHA; S6 documents the credential error/tmpdir/flock caveat. No downstream contract is broken by this child's interface.

7. Residual references — CLEAN. All cross-refs resolve to real producers/trackers: the S6 hand-off for the credential message ("see S6" → S6 AC2); `uploadpack.allowReachableSHA1InWant` and `--filter=blob:none` (real git mechanics for arbitrary-SHA fetch); `REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR` (this child introduces it, S6 AC5 documents it); `tempfile.gettempdir()`/`mkdtemp`/`fcntl.flock` (real stdlib). No dangling reference.

Note: the materialize-core module (src/rebar/_engine/repo_snapshot.py) does not yet exist in the tree (pre-manifest epic), so the analysis is grounded in the ticket-spec wording of the S1→S2 population seam, the S1→S3 snapshot-dir seam, and the S1 source_mode seam, all of which are mutually consistent.

Note (not a G4 finding on THIS pairing): the standing dependency-gap on siblings S4 (30b3)/S4b (e308) — routing the pinned-SHA snapshot handle into plan_review/attest.py's hashing path — is a code seam between S3 and S4/S4b, downstream of this child's resolved-SHA output; it does not touch the materialize-core child and is out of this pairing's scope.

Conclusion: no high-confidence cross-child inconsistency between this materialize-core child (S1) and the parent/siblings. It is the foundational PRODUCER — faithful read-tree+checkout-index materialization, atomic dir-rename population, the flock/portability seam, fetch-coalescing, descriptive credential errors, and the resolved immutable SHA — forming clean producer/consumer splits with S2 (population→cache), S2b (tmp/ residue→startup sweep; flock seam→gc/lock), S3 (snapshot dir→gate re-rooting; source_mode→local in-place), S4 (resolved SHA→signing), and S6 (credential message/tmpdir/flock caveat→docs). EMPTY G4 findings list for this pairing.
